title: Class with access modifiers
instruction:
Use `public` and `private` modifiers to control property visibility. `private` properties are only accessible inside the class.

Run this.
successMessage: `balance` is private — only `deposit()` and `getBalance()` can touch it. `owner` is public and accessible anywhere.

title: Constructor shorthand
instruction:
Prefix constructor parameters with access modifiers to automatically declare and assign class properties.

Run this.
successMessage: The `public readonly x: number` shorthand is equivalent to declaring `x`, assigning in the body, and marking it readonly. Less boilerplate.

title: Implementing an interface
instruction:
Classes can implement one or more interfaces. TypeScript verifies all required methods are present.

Run this.
successMessage: `implements Shape` means the compiler checks that `Rectangle` provides both `area()` and `perimeter()`. If you forget one, it's a compile error.

title: Inheritance
instruction:
Use `extends` to inherit from a base class. Call `super()` in the constructor to initialize the parent.

Run this.
successMessage: `Dog` overrides `speak()` from `Animal`. `super(name)` calls the parent constructor to set `this.name`.
